title: Lab-only PIO root/runtime proof path candidate

candidate_commit.subject: mmc: test A733 CMD18 PIO up to 256 blocks for diag

why_this_candidate: It is the smallest known PIO diagnostic that reached the rootfs stage.
It changes the A733 diagnostic PIO read limit from 8 blocks to 256 blocks.
It avoids inventing a new workaround while the real IDMAC failure remains unresolved.

key_lines: EXT4-fs (mmcblk0p3): mounted filesystem ro without journal
VFS: Mounted root (ext4 filesystem) readonly on device 179:3

artifact_status.note: No /srv/projects/kernel-work/outgoing manifest was found for commit 2e475c696c8b. H086 should rebuild or restage this exact candidate before the next boot.

next_procedure: On Strix, create or checkout a dedicated lab-only branch for H086 from commit 2e475c696c8b.
Build Image and sun60i-a733-cubie-a7s.dtb, preserving manifest, config, and build log.
Stage as a non-default extlinux/direct-boot artifact with bootargs: root=PARTUUID=db375e07-7682-4d4e-b8bc-a923dd0b027e rootfstype=ext4 rootwait ro rootflags=noload init=/bin/sh.
Boot Cubie3 first over UART and pass only if it reaches the read-only /bin/sh prompt.
Optionally repeat on Cubie2 after Cubie3 passes.
Restore Cubie2/Cubie3 to vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733 before ending.
guardrails: This is lab-only diagnostic infrastructure, not an upstream fix.
Do not modify public patch export from this result.
Do not hide or reclassify the normal IDMAC descriptor-fetch failure.
Do not expand into Ethernet, VPU, display, USB-C, PCIe, wireless, or Cubie1.
